feed.title: Combined summary - BIP 54 active on Bitcoin Inquisition

entry.title: Combined summary - BIP 54 active on Bitcoin Inquisition
entry.summary: The recent update to Bitcoin, known as Bitcoin Inquisition 29.2, integrates support for BIP 54 or Consensus Cleanup, which became effective from block 291168. This particular update is categorized as a bugfix soft fork and is notable for its focus on enhancing the system's reliability rather than introducing new features. Its nature as a bugfix implies that it offers limited opportunities for testing within the public Signet network. However, there was a suggestion to create a Signet block that simulates a slow transaction scenario, although not as extreme as the worst-case scenario, to test the block's validity under the new protocol. This method encourages the community to participate in sharing their findings, emphasizing the role of community engagement in assessing the effectiveness of such updates across different environments, including both public and private Signet networks. Concerns have been raised regarding the potential risk of revealing too much information about how to execute attacks on the mainnet by conducting such tests publicly. The apprehension revolves around the balance between withholding information to prevent malicious use and providing enough publicly verifiable data to support the activation proposal of a soft fork. As a precautionary measure, details were shared only in a semi-private Delving thread ([Delving Bitcoin](https://delvingbitcoin.org/t/worst-block-validation-time-inquiry/711)), which is accessible to experts likely to deduce the specifics independently. The intention behind this selective sharing is to demonstrate the feasibility of crafting slower blocks without fully disclosing the methodology for optimizing such attacks for profit or impact. This approach aims to build consensus while safeguarding the mainnet against potential vulnerabilities exposed through detailed public demonstrations. Feedback from the community remains a pivotal aspect of this process, highlighting the ongoing dialogue between maintaining security and fostering transparent development practices within the Bitcoin community.
